motif_threads: the grandmother's hat and lace collar
the title phrase 'a good man is hard to find' and its echo 'you're a good man'

character_arcs.goal: to be seen as a lady — the organizing goal of her whole life, which the story tests against the Misfit and finds has been the wrong goal
character_arcs.stakes: her self-understanding; the terms on which her life has been a life

character_arcs.goal: to reconcile a theology of Jesus-raised-the-dead with his own refusal to believe he was there to see it; to settle what he owes the world given that he cannot know
character_arcs.stakes: whether there is any pleasure in life, as he puts it at the end

nodes.sketch: the grandmother does not want to go to Florida. She reads aloud from the paper that the Misfit, a convict, has escaped and is headed that way.

motifs.motif: the grandmother's hat and lace collar

nodes.sketch: they leave for Florida anyway. the grandmother dresses for the possibility of being found dead on the road in her lace collar so strangers will know she was a lady.

motifs.motif: the grandmother's hat and lace collar

nodes.sketch: at Red Sammy's diner, the grandmother and Red Sammy agree that a good man is hard to find; the Misfit is discussed in abstract moral terms.

motifs.motif: the title phrase 'a good man is hard to find' and its echo 'you're a good man'

nodes.sketch: the grandmother remembers a plantation with a secret panel; she lies about its location being in Georgia and persuades Bailey to take a dirt road. Pitty Sing, hidden in a basket, startles.
the car turns over in a ditch. No one is seriously hurt. the grandmother realizes, as help approaches from up the road, that the plantation with the secret panel is in Tennessee, not Georgia.
a car stops. Three men get out with a gun. the grandmother recognizes the Misfit by his face from the paper.
the Misfit's two companions lead Bailey and the son into the woods; shots are heard. The mother and baby and daughter are taken next; more shots.
the grandmother, alone with the Misfit, tries to persuade him he is a good man; she offers him money; she tells him to pray.

motifs.motif: the grandmother's hat and lace collar
the title phrase 'a good man is hard to find' and its echo 'you're a good man'

nodes.sketch: she reaches out and touches the Misfit on the shoulder and says, 'Why you're one of my own children.' He springs back as if she were a snake and shoots her three times in the chest.
the Misfit, cleaning his glasses, says: 'She would of been a good woman, if it had been somebody there to shoot her every minute of her life.'

motifs.motif: the title phrase 'a good man is hard to find' and its echo 'you're a good man'

nodes.sketch: Bobby Lee says the old lady was a talker. the Misfit says there is no real pleasure in life.

motifs.motif: the grandmother's hat and lace collar

edges.realizes: the family overrides the grandmother's objection; her attempt to prevent the trip fails and she travels with a grievance
the road trip produces stops; the diner stop sets up the Misfit as a concrete presence in the moral landscape rather than a newspaper abstraction

edges.goal: to produce a memorable excursion that repairs her marginalization in the current moment
edges.stakes: her standing as the matriarch whose judgment is worth following on this trip

edges.realizes: the dirt road with the concealed cat in the basket produces exactly the kind of accident dirt roads produce; Pitty Sing's startle is the proximate physical cause
a wrecked car on a remote road attracts the attention of anyone passing; the Misfit is the one passing

edges.reframes: the newspaper-Misfit (abstract, a type) becomes the actual-Misfit (specific, in the flesh)
edges.withheld: that the Misfit and the family will intersect — the story has been staging this under the pretense of being about a tiresome family trip

edges.realizes: the Misfit's procedural calm — 'Hiram, take him and the boy into the woods' — produces the sequential murder of the family without anyone resisting
alone with the Misfit, the grandmother exhausts her repertoire — social, financial, religious — looking for the lever that will make him spare her

edges.goal: to reach the Misfit through some register she has not yet tried, having exhausted flattery and money and piety
edges.stakes: her life; and — unexpected to her — the terms on which her life has been lived

edges.realizes: the Misfit's recoil and the shots are his response to having been addressed as kin by a dying stranger; his sentence is his attempt to settle what just happened to him

edges.entails: if a person is only good when threatened (as the statement claims), then no ordinary life is a site of goodness — a conclusion the Misfit's final line reasons to from his own premises

edges.reframes: the grandmother's vanity-motivated lie about the plantation is retroactively understood as the agent of her deliverance to the moment of grace; what damned her also saved her
edges.withheld: that the detour's consequence is not punishment but confrontation with the real
